note: Source URL https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/overview-device-regulation/classify-your-medical-device confirmed live via Google index (WebFetch blocked by FDA bot-protection, not a dead URL). Page title 'Classify Your Medical Device | FDA' confirmed. 21 CFR Parts 862–892 classification structure and FD&C Act §513 framework are accurate. Three-class system description correct. NMPA three-class system and Classification Catalogue reference (国家药监局公告2017年第104号) correct. Gap description is accurate — no official cross-reference table between NMPA catalogue and FDA product codes.

Source URL https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/cdrh/cfdocs/cfPCD/classification.cfm: WebFetch returns 404 (bot-block / session-gated), but confirmed live via search results referencing the accessdata FDA product classification database tool. Three-letter product code system under 21 CFR Parts 862–892 is correct. 510(k) database URL at accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/cdrh/cfdocs/cfpmn/pmn.cfm confirmed alive (last updated 06/08/2026 per search). No NMPA–FDA product code cross-reference table: accurate.

Source URL https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/overview-device-regulation/classify-your-medical-device confirmed live (same as medus-class-001; FDA bot-blocks WebFetch). FD&C Act §513 (21 U.S.C. 360c) reference correct. Gap statement that NMPA class ≠ FDA class and that FDA classification is binding is legally accurate. SaMD guidance reference is appropriate as an example. NMPA Class II ≠ FDA Class III/I divergence point is factually accurate.

Source URL https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/premarket-submissions-selecting-and-preparing-correct-submission/premarket-notification-510k confirmed live via search (page title 'Premarket Notification 510(k) | FDA'). 21 CFR Part 807 Subpart E and 21 U.S.C. §360(k) references correct. Substantial equivalence standard accurately described. Gap items (predicate concept, 90-day review target, separate NMPA registration required) are accurate. eSTAR required for new 510(k) submissions since Oct 2023 (not mentioned in fragment — minor omission, not an error).

Source URL https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/premarket-submissions-selecting-and-preparing-correct-submission/premarket-approval-pma confirmed live via search (page title 'Premarket Approval (PMA) | FDA'). 21 CFR Part 814 and FD&C Act §515 (21 U.S.C. §360e) references correct. 180-day PMA review target is accurate. PMA vs NMPA Class III structural gap analysis is factually sound.

Source URL https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/premarket-submissions-selecting-and-preparing-correct-submission/de-novo-classification-request confirmed live via search (page title 'De Novo Classification Request | FDA'). 21 CFR Part 860 Subpart D confirmed via search (FDA issued final rule October 5, 2021 adding 21 CFR Part 860 Subpart D for De Novo). FD&C Act §513(f)(2) reference correct. China has no De Novo equivalent — confirmed. NMPA classification confirmation (分类界定) mention is appropriate.

Source URL https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/chapter-I/subchapter-H/part-820 confirmed live via search (eCFR redirects via bot-protection layer, not dead). QMSR effective date '': NOW CONFIRMED as February 2, 2026 — final rule published Feb 2, 2024 with two-year transition. CRITICAL NOTE: Row states 'FDA investigators follow QSIT' — this is now OUTDATED as of Feb 2, 2026: FDA discontinued QSIT and replaced with Compliance Program 7382.850 on Feb 2, 2026. Also: internal audits, supplier evaluations, and management review records are NOW subject to FDA inspection under QMSR (previously shielded under QSR). These changes should be updated in the source fragment but do not constitute a factual error at the time of original authorship — flag for human review. NMPA GMP (国家药监局公告2022年第55号) reference correct. ISO 13485 incorporated by reference under QMSR: confirmed.

DEAD SOURCE URL: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/chapter-I/subchapter-H/part-820/subpart-C/section-820.30 is a broken link. Under QMSR (effective Feb 2, 2026), 21 CFR Part 820 Subparts C through O are now '[Reserved]' — §820.30 as a standalone section NO LONGER EXISTS in the current eCFR. The correct current source should be: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/chapter-I/subchapter-H/part-820 (top-level Part 820 with ISO 13485:2016 incorporated by reference). STANDARD CITATION ISSUE: The row cites '21 CFR 820.30 (Design Controls, QSR)' — this section number is now obsolete/reserved under QMSR. Design control requirements are now governed by ISO 13485:2016 Clause 7.3 incorporated by reference into Part 820 QMSR. The fragment's QMSR section reference '/ QMSR Section 7.3' is conceptually correct but the citation '21 CFR 820.30' is no longer a valid standalone CFR section. FIX: Update source URL to https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/chapter-I/subchapter-H/part-820 and clarify that design controls are now governed by ISO 13485:2016 §7.3 incorporated by reference, with §820.30 retired.

Source URL https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/unique-device-identification-system-udi-system/udi-basics confirmed live via search (page title 'UDI Basics | FDA', confirmed in FDA UDI system section). 21 CFR Part 801 and Part 830 references correct. UDI Rule (78 FR 58786, 2013) date correct — published September 24, 2013. FD&C Act §502 reference for misbranding is correct. UDI issuing agencies (GS1, HIBCC, ICCBBA) correct. Gap analysis on language, UDI system divergence, and label content differences is accurate. NMPA Order No. 6 (2021) for labeling: confirmed correct citation.

Source URL https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/how-study-and-market-your-device/device-registration-and-listing confirmed live via search (page title 'Device Registration and Listing | FDA'). 21 CFR Part 807 annual registration window October 1 – December 31 confirmed correct. FURLS system confirmed. 21 U.S.C. §331 (prohibited acts for failure to register) confirmed correct. FY2026 MDUFA registration fee confirmed at $11,423 (not stated in fragment, which is correct in not specifying an amount). Gap description accurate — no NMPA equivalent annual establishment re-registration obligation.

Source URL https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/chapter-I/subchapter-H/part-807/subpart-C/section-807.40 confirmed live via search (eCFR page title '21 CFR 807.40 -- Establishment registration and device listing for foreign establishments importing or offering for import devices into the United States'). 21 CFR §807.40 content confirmed: single US agent designation, US residency requirement, must assist FDA with inspections and communications, 10 business-day change notification. 21 U.S.C. §360(b)(2) reference correct. Gap description (no Chinese domestic counterpart) is accurate.

Source URL https://www.fda.gov/industry/fda-user-fee-programs/medical-device-user-fee-amendments-mdufa confirmed live via search (page title 'Medical Device User Fee Amendments (MDUFA) | FDA'). FY2026 establishment registration fee confirmed at $11,423 (fragment correctly leaves amount as '' and directs to FDA schedule — appropriate). Unpaid fee = 'Not Registered' status and inadmissible devices: confirmed accurate. 21 U.S.C. §379j reference correct. No NMPA annual establishment-level fee equivalent: confirmed accurate.
